mission.title: oras: Add new commands to support uploading and downloading images
mission.description: Add new commands to support uploading and downloading images. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current oras deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oras version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
helm list -n oras 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oras installation.

steps.title: Review oras configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oras configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras
```
### What is the version of your ORAS CLI

oras v1.1.0

### What would you like to be added?

Had an offline discussion with  and he suggests to add two commands:
- `oras backup` (alias: `snapshot`, `pull-image`) to support saving an image from

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add new commands to support uploading and downloading images
steps.description:
Specification for the way the `oras backup` and `oras restore` commands will work.
Partial: https://github.com/oras-project/oras/issues/1366
```yaml
- `oras restore` (alias: `push-image`) to support pushing an image from an OCI image layout tar archive to remote registry.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add new commands to support uploading and downloading images" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Specification for the way the `oras backup` and `oras restore` commands will work.
Partial: https://github.com/oras-project/oras/issues/1366
codeSnippets: - `oras restore` (alias: `push-image`) to support pushing an image from an OCI image layout tar archive to remote registry.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oras installed or the issue environment reproducible.
